medical_history.chief_complaint: Pathologically increasing head circumference and symptoms of increased intracranial pressure, in combination with marked developmental delay and loss of abilities
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: At 1 month of age, she underwent an echocardiogram revealing a persistent foramen ovale but no cardiac insufficiency and also had two suspected left partial epileptic seizures. At 2 months, developmental delay was noted with lack of eye contact, no smiles, and feeding difficulties, and at 3.5 months, an increase in head circumference was noted. At 6 months of age, she had opisthotonus-like attacks with an increasing frequency. She also kept her head extended when placed in bed, all signs corresponding to symptoms of increased intracranial pressure.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Persistent foramen ovale, suspected left partial epileptic seizures, developmental delay, feeding difficulties
medical_history.family_history: Mother with similar cutaneous capillary haemangiomas and RASA1 mutation

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Radiological evaluation with transfonatanellar ultrasound and MRI revealed a vein of Galen malformation with hydrocephalus, and tonsillar prolapse. Angiographic examination revealed a mural type of vein of Galen malformation with its main supply from the postero-lateral and postero-medial choroidal arteries. There was a pronounced hypoplasia of the superior sagittal sinus compensated by enlarged cortical veins draining mainly into the cavernous sinus. Persisting occipital and marginal sinuses were noted and there was a high-grade stenosis of the right jugular bulb.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Mutation in exon 16 of the RASA1 gene (c.2125C > T)
Mutation in exon 21 of the RASA1 gene (c.2707C > T)
Mutation in exon 9 of the RASA1 gene (c.1279C > T)

radiographic_examinations.findings: Vein of Galen malformation with hydrocephalus, and tonsillar prolapse
Vein of Galen malformation with hydrocephalus, and tonsillar prolapse
Mural type of vein of Galen malformation with its main supply from the postero-lateral and postero-medial choroidal arteries
High-flow shunts at the level of the superior sagittal sinus
Large dural sinus malformation with multiple fistulous communications between meningeal arteries and the malformed superior sagittal sinus itself
Malformation of the superior sagittal sinus with associated high-flow fistulas
Bilobulated venous ectasia close to the midline with dominant arterial supply arising from the posterior circulation with normal brain parenchyma
Direct arteriovenous fistula between along a circumferential artery and an ectatic tectal vein that secondarily drained towards the dilated vein of Galen

other_tests.findings: Persistent foramen ovale but no cardiac insufficiency
Considered normal despite some MR signal abnormalities in the brain white matter
No structural cardiac anomaly but significant cardiac insufficiency with cardiac enlargement, and pulmonary hypertension requiring assisted ventilation

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Capillary malformation–arteriovenous malformation syndrome with RASA1 mutation
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was confirmed through genetic testing revealing a mutation in the RASA1 gene, which is known to cause capillary malformation–arteriovenous malformation syndrome. The presence of intracranial high-flow arteriovenous shunts and cutaneous capillary malformations supported the diagnosis.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: acrylic glue used to embolize two different branches of the right postero-lateral choroidal artery
